Elon’s small lead of 600 into the 1500 pot is consistent with a wide, often top-pair or probing C-bet range; Flash checked and is still live. With trips I want value and protection (charge A-x, pocket pairs and any backdoor spade holdings) and I want to build the pot in position. The minimum raise (to 1200) is too small — it gives free or cheap equity to draws and doesn’t extract enough from Ax/PP. I’ll raise to 3000 total to get value and charge draws while leaving room to navigate a larger 3-bet; plan is to continue for value on safe turns and re-evaluate versus big aggression (likely calling/mucking only to extreme overbets but leaning to call a reasonable 3-bet with a set).

I have King-high on an Ace-high board with no significant draws. The action ahead of me is extremely aggressive: the Big Blind (Elon) led into the field, and the Button (Minni) put in a massive raise to 3000. My hand has no chance of winning this pot without an improbable sequence of events, and calling such a large amount with nothing is mathematically incorrect. I will fold.

Facing Minni's flop raise to 3000 after my 600 c-bet into a multiway pot on A♠4♠2♦ board. Holding T♠7♠ for nut spade flush draw (9 outs, ~35% equity). Pot 4500, calling 2400 more (pot odds ~34.8% required equity, which nut FD achieves vs. her polarized range of value like A♠x, sets, or semi-bluffs with other spades/wheel draws). Minni's button preflop raise is wide per history (frequent raiser, calls big pots speculatively as in H2, H17); her large 5x raise over small bet smells polarized (strong hands or bluffs). Effective stacks deep for me (24k), but her ~4700 behind post-raise creates low SPR (~1.1 after call), committing us both—ideal for realizing my massive equity OOP. Folding squanders equity in BB defense spot; raising risks stacking off to better (e.g., A♠K♠) while folding out bluffs. History shows Minni checks down monsters sometimes (H4, H6), so call preserves fold equity on turn if bricked, jam any spade or strong turn improvement. Standard play maximizes EV.

Heads-up on turn with Ts7s nut spade flush draw (9 outs, ~18.6% equity to river). Pot 6900, OOP acting first vs Minni (4700 behind on button). Flop call was correct with deep stacks and low SPR post-call (~1.1), but turn Qh bricks completely—no spade, no straight help. Her flop raise to 3000 over my 600 c-bet polarizes her range: strong Ax (esp A♠x), sets (AA unlikely, 44/22 possible), other spades/semi-bluffs, wheel draws. Qh is neutral-ish (pairs weak Qx, doesn't complete wheel). Betting risks getting jammed by better (SPR low, pot-committing) while folding out her bluffs/missed draws; poor EV given OOP position and no improvement. Raising uncalled flop bet commits stacks poorly with pure draw. Check best: pot control OOP, charges her bluffs/value bets, realizes equity if she checks behind (history shows Minni passive postflop sometimes, e.g., H4, H6, H12 checks monsters), gets to river free ~half time. If bet, call small (pot odds good) but fold jam (need ~29% equity for all-in call, have ~18-35% vs polarized range but OOP dynamics favor folding). Plan: check, call small bet/light jam if odds hold, fold heavy action to preserve stack for better spots.

I flopped a set of 4s (4h4d on 4s As 2d) and built the pot by raising the flop to 3,000; Elon called, so he has a relatively strong or sticky calling range (lots of A-x, pocket pairs, and spade draws). On the turn a Qh arrived and he checked — the Q improves some of his hands (A-Q, QQ) but most of his calling range (top pair A, Qx, pairs, and draws) is still behind my set. I want value and to charge spade draws and A-x hands while leaving some room to maneuver on the river, so I’ll bet ~half the pot. Betting 3,500 into 6,900 balances getting called by worse (A-x, Qx, middling pairs, and redraws) while not committing my entire 4,700 stack unnecessarily. If he check-raises big I’ll strongly consider calling/jamming with trips (only very unlikely quads or better trips beat me), and if he just calls I’ll plan to extract further on the river or snap-call large bets — but for now put in 3,500 to charge and extract.

On the turn Qh, a neutral brick that doesn't improve my nut spade flush draw (9 outs, ~19% equity to river) but doesn't complete obvious draws for villain except wheel (unlikely). Pot 10400, facing Minni's 3500 bet (~1/3 pot), requiring ~25% equity to call directly, but her 1200 chips behind creates reverse implied odds risk yet excellent forward implied if I hit (nearly pot-committed post-call, SPR ~0.09 for her). Her range polarized from flop overraise: value (sets like 44/22/Ax especially A♠x), missed spades/wheel draws bluffing. Vs value ~20-35% equity (flush beats weak Ax, loses to sets), vs bluffs ~50-100%; blended >25% threshold. History shows Minni aggressive pre/flop but passive OOP sometimes (checks monsters H4/H6); OOP check-call strategy preserves stack flexibility while realizing massive equity in low-SPR spot. Calling flop was +EV with deep stacks; folding now realizes no equity after investment. Raising OOP with pure draw risks isolating vs better (e.g., A♠x jams), lower EV than flat (pot odds crush if she jams 1200 river ~7% needed). Post-call, she'll likely jam (9% pot), offering fold equity denial but my 18050 behind handles jam easily (1200/16300 ~7% needed >>19%). Plan: call, jam any spade/straight river (unlikely), fold only to unlikely check-behind jam.

River Ah pairs the board, improving value hands in Minni's strong polarized range from the flop overraise and continued turn betting (Ax now two-pair or better, sets to full houses, AA trips). My Ts7s missed the nut flush draw entirely, bricking on Qh turn and Ah river—no pair, straight draw, or showdown value; pure air in a bloated pot (13900). Already invested significantly preflop/flop/turn, but sunk cost fallacy avoided; folding preserves 18050 stack for superior spots over 127 remaining hands. First to act OOP vs aggressive Minni (1200 behind), checking is mandatory—no thin value, low fold equity bluff bet given her commitment (SPR <0.1, she'll call light with top pair+ or even draws). If she bets 1200 all-in, ~7% pot odds needed but 0% equity = instant fold. History confirms Minni bets big with strength (H3, H21) but bluffs rarely OOP; check-fold extracts max EV by avoiding reverse implied/fold equity denial.

Board: 4♠ A♠ 2♦ Q♥ A♥ — with 4♥4♦ in hand my best five is 4♥4♦4♠A♠A♥: a full house (fours full of aces). This is an extremely strong river hand. The only hands that realistically beat me are very narrow: quads with both remaining aces or full houses where villain holds A4 or A2 (a handful of combos). Elon’s line (lead/call on the flop, check-call on the turn) is textbook for lots of A-x and medium pairs — many of those hands are now trips or inferior and will call a small value bet. The pot is huge (13,900) and I only have 1,200 behind; shoving is effectively a small value bet that will be called by worse hands and maximizes value from Ax and medium pairs. Checking surrenders value to showdown. I will bet my remaining stack (all-in).

River Ah pairs the ace, massively improving Minni's polarized range from the flop overraise and turn bet: Ax now makes trips or better (e.g., A♠x nuts earlier), sets boat up, while my T♠7♠ missed the nut flush draw entirely—no pair, straight outs (gutshot dead vs. paired board), or any showdown value. Pot 15100 with 1200 to call offers ~7-8% equity needed, but pure air has 0% vs. her strong value-heavy range (history shows aggressive lines with strength like H3/H21, rarely pure bluff OOP river). Sunk costs from prior calls irrelevant; folding preserves 18050 stack (~120BB effective) for 127 remaining hands vs. spew. Check-fold pre-bet was plan; facing bet, instant fold maximizes EV long-term.

I am on the button with Ace-Seven offsuit. My opponents are Elon (SB) and Minni (BB). Hand #23 showed Minni playing very aggressively and winning a large pot from Elon, so both players have significant stacks and are active. Ace-Seven offsuit is a standard opening hand on the button in a three-handed game to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot. I will raise to 300 to build the pot and potentially win it pre-flop.

Pre-flop in SB with 78c suited connectors, a speculative hand with good playability for straights and flushes. Facing a Button raise to 300 from Flash (likely wide range), pot is 450, calling 250 more offers decent implied odds with deep stacks (mine 18k, Minni 15k). SB defends wide vs BTN opens, especially suited connectors. Multi-way potential with BB. Post-flop position is tough but hand realizes equity well. Folding too tight OOP; 3-betting not ideal without strong blockers. Call to realize equity and control pot OOP.

Facing a button open to 300 with the small blind calling, I'm in a 3-way pot out of position with Qc8s. This hand is easily dominated by typical BTN open and SB calling ranges and has poor post‑flop playability multiway. The price to call (200 into a 700 pot) gives ~22% required equity, but multiway and OOP my realistic equity and ability to extract value are lower. A light 3‑bet to isolate is unattractive here (I'd still be out of position and a small 3‑bet won't fold enough hands to justify bloating the pot with Q8o). Fold and preserve chips for a better spot.

Flop is 3s-Ks-3d, which completely whiffs my 78cc suited connectors. I have no pair, no straight draw (78 doesn't connect with the board for any straight or gutshot), and no flush draw since the board has spades and diamonds but I'm clubs. Pure air with no showdown value or draws. Heads-up OOP vs Button raiser who likely continuation bets wide on this paired low board with a king overcard. Check-fold is optimal; no reason to donk bet air or bloat pot. Realize the hand didn't connect as hoped post-flop realization plan, cut losses with deep stacks but realize Flash shallower at 6.5k.

I'll make a continuation bet on this paired board. The King-high flop with a pair of threes is generally better for my range as the pre-flop aggressor. Elon's check is a standard response for a caller on this texture. A bet of 300 into the 700 pot is a standard sizing to put pressure on his non-pairing hands and potentially take down the pot immediately while maintaining control with my Ace-high.

Flop is 3s-Ks-3d, pairing treys with a king spade overcard. My 78cc suited connectors completely misses: no pair, no straight draw (78 doesn't connect for open-ender or gutshot), no flush draw (clubs vs spades/diamond board). Pure bluff-catcher at best but zero showdown value OOP heads-up vs Button c-bet after their pre-flop raise. Pot 1000, facing 300 (30% pot bet, small but standard wide c-bet sizing on this dry paired board). Flash likely bets wide from BTN range including Ax, Kx, broadways, pairs. Calling builds pot OOP with no equity; raising as pure bluff risky given board favors value (trips possible, Kx crushes) and my lack of blockers. Deep effective stacks but no implied odds with air. Pre-flop call was for multi-way speculative playability, but whiffed hard—fold to realize plan and avoid spew. Prioritize discipline over curiosity.

Qh9h is a premium suited connector on the button facing limps (pot 150, to call 100, likely SB limp and BB check). Excellent position, great playability, equity against wide limp/call ranges. Raising isolates weaker OOP players, builds pot for value, steals dead money. Standard size around 4x BB with limp: 400 total. Deep stacks support aggression. Minni and Flash have shown wide ranges in history; exploit this.

I'm in the small blind with A♠8♠ facing a button open to 400 in a 3-handed pot. Folding is too weak with a suited ace and calling simply concedes the initiative and leaves me OOP vs a wide button opening range. I want to apply pressure, use my ace-blockers, and either take the pot now or play a heads-up pot with a strong hand. I 3-bet to ~3x the open (1,200 total) to isolate the button, gain fold equity, and set a comfortable postflop stack-to-pot ratio with deep stacks. If I face a large 4-bet I will re-evaluate (likely fold to very large 4-bets); if called I'll c-bet appropriate ace/spade-favorable boards and play cautiously on heavy action.

I am in the Big Blind with 9c7c, a decent suited connector. Elon (Button) opened to 400, and Minni (Small Blind) 3-bet to 1200. This 3-bet from the SB into a button open is typically quite strong, especially given Minni's recent aggressive play. Calling 1100 into a pot of 1700 (odds of ~1.5:1) while being out of position against two strong opponents—one of whom has shown considerable aggression—is a losing proposition with a marginal hand like 97s. Folding allows me to preserve my stack and wait for a better spot to enter a pot.

Facing a 3-bet from Minni in the SB after my button open to 400. Qh9h is a strong suited connector with great playability, position, and equity (35-45% vs typical 3-bet ranges like TT+, AJo+, KQs+). Pot odds are favorable: calling 800 into ~2500 effective pot (~3:1 direct odds, plus deep implied odds ~80BB effective). Minni's history shows wide raising/calling ranges (e.g., Hand 2 calling massive river bets, Hand 9 5-bet jamming light?), but often passive postflop/checks. Postflop button advantage vs OOP SB is massive for realizing equity, controlling pot, bluffing turns/rivers. Folding too tight early; 4-betting risks too much (~2-3x pot) with speculative hand better multiway/postflop. Call to play pot in position, exploit Minni's calling station tendencies while keeping pot manageable pre.

I have top two pair (A♠ on the board + my A♥ and the two Jacks on the board = Aces and Jacks). This is a very strong hand heads-up but vulnerable to any Jack in villain’s hand (trips/quads) or pocket/paired Aces (full house) / AJ (full house). Elon called my 3‑bet preflop, so his continuing range includes Ax, broadways and medium/strong pairs — there are some Jx and AA/AJ combos but they are relatively uncommon. I want thin value from worse Aces and medium pairs and I want to deny free cards that can give him trips or a full house on later streets. Betting around 2/3 pot maximizes value from worse Ax and pocket pairs while controlling the pot size against the small portion of his range that dominates me. Pot is 2,500 so I will bet 1,700. If he raises large I will assume a very strong hand (Jx/AA/AJ) and fold; if he calls I will re-evaluate the turn for further value or pot control.

Flop board Js As Jh is extremely strong and coordinated: paired Jacks, Ace high, spade flush draw. My Qh9h is complete air—no pair, no straight draw (Q9 doesn't connect), backdoor heart flush draw at best but irrelevant. Facing SB's 3-bet preflop followed by a 1700 donk bet (~2/3 pot) into 2500 preflop pot, this line screams strength (JJ set, AJ two-pair/top pair, AK/AQ, spade draws with backdoors). Equity realization low vs value (<20%), even vs polarized maybe 25-30%, but pot odds 1700 into 5900 (~25% needed) marginal with poor implied odds ahead (SPR ~2.5 post-call, no showdown value). Position allows floating, but Minni's history (wide but often passive OOP, yet aggressive here) suggests she'll continue strong; raising bluff risks stacks with no equity. Best to fold clean air early, preserve deep stack (16k) for 125+ hands remaining—discipline over speculative plays.

Button in a 3-handed pot — 6♦5♥ is a playable hand because I have position and good opportunity to steal. I want to apply pressure and avoid letting the blinds see a cheap flop, so I’ll open with a standard 3x sized raise to 300. This sizing is consistent with my table image and reduces multiway pots with marginal holdings. Postflop plan: continuation-bet many favorable/dry boards, proceed cautiously on high-card/connected/flushy textures, and fold to large 3-bets (only consider calling small 3-bets for position/implied odds).

I am in the Small Blind with King of Hearts and Nine of Clubs (K9o). Minni, on the button, has raised to 300. While K9o is a marginal hand against a standard open, this table has shown quite a bit of pre-flop aggression. However, my stack is around 71 BB (7100), and I'm out of position against two deep stacks (Minni and Elon). Calling out of the small blind often leads to difficult post-flop situations. I've been playing relatively tight recently. Given the 3x raise from the button, I could potentially 3-bet as a bluff/semi-bluff, but K9o doesn't block much of Minni's likely continuing range besides some K-high hands. Folding is the safest and most disciplined play here to preserve my stack for better spots where I have position or a stronger hand.

Preflop in BB facing button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot 450, calling 200 more for ~2.25:1 direct odds, needing ~31% equity. 5s3s is a suited gapped connector with flush and wheel straight potential. Against Minni's wide button opening range (likely 40-50% of hands given history of aggression), it has ~38-42% equity raw, plus strong implied odds with deep effective stacks (~16k). OOP postflop but pot odds too good to fold, and playability decent for check-calling/calling down light or check-raising draws. Folding too tight; 3-bet not ideal without blockers. Call to realize equity and defend.

Heads-up on Jc7c4h flop OOP in BB vs button raiser Minni. Hold 5s3s for gutshot straight draw (4 outs to 3-4-5-6-7, ~8% equity). No pair, no flush draw. Pot 650 deep stacks (~16k effective). Board coordinated with club flush draw, rainbow otherwise. Minni's button opening range wide (40-50% per history: aggressive preflop, continuation bets often seen). Raw equity ~35-40% vs her range due to backdoor potential, blockers, and playability. OOP first to act: check standard to pot control, realize equity passively, induce bluffs/checks, or get good call odds on small c-bets (pot odds favorable for 8%+ draws). Betting out too thin for value/protection (weak draw, foldout better hands); folding preflop was tight given odds. Check to maintain balance, exploit aggression by check-calling light, set up check-raise if she reps strength with air/draws.

I opened from the button and Elon (BB) just checked. With 6♦5♥ on Jc-7c-4h I have an open-ended straight draw (3s or 8s — 8 outs, ~31% to hit by river) and a tiny backdoor heart possibility. In position as the preflop raiser this is a textbook semi-bluff spot: the board is relatively dry and a half‑pot c-bet will fold a lot of unpaired overcards and weaker holdings in his calling range while giving me good equity if called. I’ll size to ~1/2 pot to keep fold equity and pot control. Plan: fold to a large raise (this is a semi-bluff), call small resistance to realize equity and play turns in position, and if I hit on turn/river extract value.

Facing ~1/2 pot c-bet (325 into 650 pre-bet pot, now 975 total) OOP with 53s gutshot straight draw (4 outs to 3-4-5-6-7, ~8%) + backdoor spade flush/straight potential on Jc7c4h board. Pot odds: 325 to call into 975 (need ~25% equity). Hand retains ~35-40% equity vs Minni's wide button opening + c-bet range (aggressive history: frequent preflop raises, flop cbets even on misses/draws as seen in hands 3,6,15,19,23). Deep effective stacks (~15.6k) provide implied odds for when 6 hits (nuts or strong 5-high str8, stacks off possible vs overpairs/Jx). OOP check-call strategy balances range, induces bluffs/checks from air, exploits her aggression without overcommitting. Raising adds unnecessary variance/risk (low fold equity vs sticky player, committed post-raise); folding too tight given price/playability. Call flop, pot control turn if miss (fold to large bet), stack-off if improve.

Turn board Ac completes the nut club flush draw on an already coordinated Jc7c4h board, adding significant danger (possible flushes, Ax pairs, straights). Holding 5s3s for only a gutshot straight draw to the 6 (~9% equity to river, 4 outs) plus negligible backdoor spade flush/straight outs, no pair or flush. Pot 1300, deep effective stacks (~15.6k). OOP first to act vs Minni's wide button opening + flop c-bet range (aggressive history: frequent cbets on draws/misses as in hands 3,6,15,19,23; defends light). Raw equity ~28-33% vs her polarized range (value: sets, two-pair, overpairs, Ax/Jx; bluffs/draws: clubs, gutshots like 65/85, air). Preflop/flop OOP check-call was correct to realize equity at good odds (~2:1 flop call needing 25% with ~35-40% equity then). Now, betting out too thin for value (weak draw folds out her Ax/flushes), low fold equity vs sticky caller (history of calling down like hand 2), adds variance OOP. Folding premature (25%+ equity baseline, implied odds if hit nuts-ish straight). Check standard: pot control OOP, induces her bluffs/checks from missed draws/overpairs scared of flush/Ace, balances check-call range (sets/flushes/draws), sets up river decision (fold most bets if miss unless tiny). Exploits her aggression by check-calling light rivers if improves/misses. If she bets small, favorable odds to chase (~33% needed for half-pot); large bet, good fold. Check preserves stack, realizes equity passively.

I have 6♦5♥ — an OESD from the flop that now has 8 outs to hit the river (≈17.4% on the river). Elon checked the turn after A♣ — that card helps his calling/3‑betting range more than it helps my c‑betting range, and it reduces my fold equity versus hands that already beat me (Ax, two pair, sets). Betting the turn risks a raise that I can’t profitably call with only draw equity. With position and a single-card draw I should conserve chips and realize equity for free. I’ll check and evaluate the river: if I complete the straight I’ll bet for value; if I miss I’ll fold to meaningful pressure and only consider calling very small bluff bets.

River 5c pairs the board's 5 (giving me bottom pair with 5s) on a highly coordinated Jc7c4h-Ac-5c board with four clubs, screaming nut flushes (any club in hand), strong Ax/Jx/sets, or two-pair combos. Previously OOP check-called flop gutshot (~35% equity realizing passively) and checked turn after Ac flush card, which Minni checked behind—suggesting possible missed draw, overpair scared of board texture, or trapping value (but less likely given check-back). Pot 1300, deep stacks (~15.6k effective), first to act OOP. Pair of 5s has showdown value vs her wide button open + flop cbet + turn check range (air/missed clubs/gutshots like 65/86/28, overpairs KQ+/AJ without c redraw, Jx weak kicker), but crushed by any flush/Ax/set. Betting thin value risky: low fold equity vs sticky/aggressive Minni (history calling down light, e.g., hand 2), invites raises from better, folds out worse; doesn't protect. Raising irrelevant. Check optimal: pot control OOP, induces bluffs/checks from weaker hands (exploits aggression/check-back tendency in history, e.g., hands 4,6,7,12,17), balances check range (draws/monsters), free showdown likely (she checked turn, pot small). If she bets small, call light (pair decent vs polarized range); large bet, good fold. Maximizes EV by realizing equity passively without bloating pot OOP.

Board: J♣ 7♣ 4♥ A♣ 5♣. My hand 6♦5♥ = a weak pair of fives with no club. The river added the 4th club to the board, so any single club in Elon’s hand now makes a flush that beats me. He called my flop c‑bet (range: Jx, 7x, pocket pairs, single‑club holdings, some Ax) then checked the turn and checked the river. Given the line and the board texture, a large portion of his calling range now beats me (any club, any Ace, Jx, two pairs, sets). Betting the river for value is thin — most hands that will call are better (and bluffs that would fold are rarer now), and bluffing has very little fold equity vs this opponent’s range. Best play is to check to showdown and only call a very small river bet; fold to meaningful pressure.

I am on the button with J9o, which is a decent but not premium hand in a 3-handed game. Usually, a raise from the button is standard to put pressure on the blinds. However, looking at the previous hands, Elon (SB) and Minni (BB) have both been quite active and aggressive. In this specific spot, raising to 300 is a standard steal attempt. If I get re-raised, I can comfortably fold, and if called, J9o has some playability on various board textures. Given the stack depths and the 3-handed nature of the game, I will raise to 3x the big blind.

3-handed, Button raise to 3bb, SB defense should be wide but T8o is offsuit with poor playability OOP. No strong blockers or suited equity. BB still to act and could 3-bet or call wide based on history. Folding marginal hands preserves stack for better spots; EV negative long-term OOP with this hand.

Flash opened to 300 from the button (3x). I’m in the big blind with A♥5♣ — a marginal but defendable hand: ace blocker, usable as a bluff-catcher and to hit top-pair. Facing a standard button open and being out of position, I prefer to call and keep the pot controllable rather than making a small 3-bet that will be called a lot or induce a 4-bet. Calling 200 more (300 total committed) gives me reasonable pot odds to see a flop against what should be a fairly wide opening range. Plan: fold to a large 4-bet preflop; postflop continue only with top pair/strong draws or favorable runouts, otherwise control pot and fold to heavy pressure.

I have top pair with a weak kicker (A♥5♣) on 4♠7♠A♦. This is exactly the kind of hand I want to continue with — it has showdown value, blocks some A-x combos, and I need to charge spade draws and worse aces/small pairs. I’m heads-up and first to act on the flop, so a value/protection c-bet is appropriate. A half‑pot bet (~325 into 650) balances getting called by worse A‑x and small pairs while making spade draws and speculative hands pay. If called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (pot‑control vs continuing based on the card and opponent sizing); I’ll fold to a large check‑raise/shove that represents a better ace or set, but will call modest resistance. This line follows my preflop plan to continue with top pair but keep the pot manageable against heavy pressure.

I opened pre-flop with J9o from the button and was called by Minni in the big blind. The flop comes Ad-7s-4s, and Minni leads out for 325 into a 650 pot. This 'donk bet' often represents a weak Ace, a piece of the board, or a spade draw. I have no pair, no draw, and no backdoor equity on this board. Continuing with a bluff raise is risky against an active player who has shown interest in the pot, and calling with Jack-high is not viable. Folding is the most disciplined and profitable play here.

3-handed on the button with deep stacks, my range should be very wide for opens, around 50% of hands or more. 2d4s is suited with straight potential (wheel) and flush draw possibility, great post-flop playability in position. Blinds are loose/passive from history (lots of calling stations), but stealing 150 pot (1.5BB) with fold equity is +EV. Standard raise size 3x BB to 300 to build pot without overcommitting. Will fold to 4bet, play post-flop if called.

I’m in the small blind with T7o facing a button open to 300 and the big blind still to act. This hand has poor playability out of position and is often dominated by a BTN opening range; calling 250 to play OOP into a likely multiway pot is a marginal, low-expected-value spot. A tiny reraise to 500 is too light — it won’t credibly fold better hands and risks a 3-bet from the button or getting isolated with a worse hand postflop. With deep stacks and a 150-hand session ahead, I’ll conserve chips for better, positionally-favorable spots. Fold.

I am in the big blind with J3 offsuit. Elon, the button, has raised to 3x. While I only need to call 200 more into a pot of 450, J3o is a very weak hand to play out of position against a deep-stacked aggressive opponent. Looking at previous hands, Elon is active and has shown willingness to push pots. Defending with such a marginal hand from the big blind against a button raise often leads to difficult post-flop situations where I am likely dominated or forced to fold. Conserving my stack for better spots is the more prudent professional play here.

On the button with 2♠T♦ I have a marginal but playable hand — the key is position. The table's standard open size has been around 3bb, and raising gives me fold equity vs the blinds and a chance to play postflop in position. I will open to 300 (total) — large enough to discourage light defenses but small relative to my stack. Plan: fold to a sizable 3-bet, call small 3-bets occasionally given position, and play cautiously postflop (c-bet on favorable textures, check/peel on dry or dominated boards).

I am in the Small Blind with K7o. Minni has opened from the Button to 3BB (300). K7o is a marginal hand to play out of position against a loose and aggressive player like Minni, especially with a short-ish stack of 66.5BB. Folding is very reasonable to avoid difficult post-flop spots, and calling invites the Big Blind (Elon) into the pot. Given the previous hands, Minni is quite active. However, K7o doesn't play well enough post-flop to justify a call here, and a 3-bet would be a high-variance bluff. I'll opt for a fold.

2c7s is one of the worst possible hands in poker, completely uncoordinated and low. Facing a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive but this hand has no playability. Pot odds are 200 to call into 450 (roughly 3:1), but equity against a typical button opening range (~35-40%) is abysmal (~25-30%), and I'll be OOP post-flop with no initiative. Folding preserves stack for premium spots in a long 150-hand game.

I have been dealt Ace-King offsuit (AKo) on the button, which is a premium starting hand. The pot is currently 150 with the small and big blinds. As the button, I have position for the rest of the hand. Standard play is to raise to gain initiative and build the pot. A 3x big blind raise (300) is standard in this game.

78o is a marginal hand with some straight potential but offsuit and poor playability out of position. Facing a button raise from Flash, who raises frequently based on history, the standard play is to fold from the small blind to avoid difficult post-flop spots OOP with a weak hand. Pot odds are not favorable enough (calling 250 into 450), and my stack is deep enough to wait for better opportunities. Preserving stack for value spots.

I will call. I’m in the big blind with J♥T♦ facing a button open to 300. The pot is 450 and I need to call 200 (300 total), which gives me ~30.8% pot-odds — JTo has adequate equity vs a typical button opening range heads-up. Out of position, I’d rather keep the pot manageable than 3-bet with a marginal hand (a min 3-bet to 500 is too small to apply real pressure and risks awkward 4-bet spots). With both stacks deep enough to play postflop, I’ll call to see a flop and then proceed: continue/aggress when I connect (pair, two pair, strong draws), control the pot or fold to heavy aggression when I miss. Calling also keeps my range balanced in this spot.

Check. On 7♣6♣2♠ with J♥T♦ I have two overcards and 6 outs to pair (≈12.8% on the turn, ≈24% by the river) but no club backdoor. The board is relatively dry except for club draws, and I’m out of position vs a button raiser — I prefer pot control and to realize equity rather than c-bet and inflate the pot OOP with a marginal hand. Plan: check and call a reasonable turn bet, fold to heavy aggression if I miss, and apply pressure or bet for value if I pair up or pick up a strong draw on later streets.

I was the pre-flop aggressor with AKo. The flop of 7c-6c-2s is relatively low and favors the Big Blind's calling range, as it contains more sets and two-pair combinations than my range. However, Minni checked to me, and I still hold two overcards with high equity against her non-pairing hands. I will place a standard continuation bet of 300 (roughly half the pot) to maintain initiative, fold out her air, and protect my hand against various draws.

I’m out of position with J♥T♦ on 7♣6♣2♠ facing a small c-bet (300 into a 950 pot). Calling is the best line: the bet is small so my required equity is only ~19.4% (300 to win ~1550), and JTo has enough equity vs a wide button c-betting range to justify a call. I want to keep the pot controllable OOP and realize my equity with two overcards and backdoor straight possibilities rather than inflate the pot by raising with a marginal hand. Plan: call the 300 to see a turn, fold to heavy turn aggression if I completely miss, but bluff/raise or apply pressure if I pick up a pair or a strong draw. Therefore I’ll call.

Flash checked the turn after c-betting the flop. I have J♥T♦ — two overcards, no club backdoor and only ~6 outs to pair (~13% to hit the river). Out of position with a marginal hand, I want to keep the pot controllable rather than inflate it with a thin bluff. Checking lets me realize equity, see the river card, and make a clearer decision (call small river bets, fold to heavy pressure, or bet/raise only if I pair or pick up a real draw). Therefore I check.

The turn card is a 9d, which doesn't change the board much but adds some potential straight draws for Minni's range. I have Ace-King high and was the pre-flop aggressor and continuation-bettor. Minni has checked twice. While AK still has value, the board (7-6-2-9) is very coordinated for a Big Blind calling range. If I bet again and get raised, I'm in a difficult spot. By checking behind, I control the size of the pot, realize my equity, and can potentially induce a bluff on the river or catch a pair that would be the best hand. Checking is the most balanced play here to avoid over-committing with just high cards.

I made top pair on the river (J♥T♦ on 7♣6♣2♠9♦J♣). Flash open-shoved a standard button range, c‑bet the flop and then checked the turn and river — a line consistent with a wide, medium-strength/missed-draw range (overcards, small pairs, club draws that missed). The pot is 1,250 — a small value bet (~1/3 pot) asks for a call of ~400 to win 1,650, so the break-even caller frequency is only ~24%. That’s easily reached by worse hands (9x, 7x, 6x, 2x, many Tx/overcard showdown calls and some missed draws), while a check-raise would usually represent two pair/sets or a better J and I can fold those. With deep stacks and low risk, a small value bet is +EV. I will bet 400 to extract value from worse hands and deny a free showdown to some hands that would call a small river bet.

On the river, the board is 7c-6c-2s-9d-Jc. I hold Ace-King high (AsKd). I was the pre-flop aggressor and continuation-bettor on the flop. After checking the turn, Minni leads out for 400 into a pot of 1250 on the river. The Jack of Clubs completes some flush draws (though mostly backdoors) and gives some straight draws (like T8) a higher card, but overall, it's a card that could hit Minni's range if she had a Jack. However, 400 is a relatively small bet (under 1/3 pot). Given my King of Diamonds doesn't block any club draws, it's possible she is betting a missed straight draw or a weaker pair (like a 6 or 7) for value/protection. Ace-King is often the best hand here against a wide range of 'prodding' bets, and the price offered (400 to win 1650) makes a call mathematically viable to prevent being bluffed off the best hand.

Preflop on the button with T8o heads-up vs BB after SB folded. Pot 150 with dead SB money. T8o is a strong enough hand in position heads-up, with good playability postflop (straight potential, decent high card). Standard play is to raise for value, steal equity, and build pot with position advantage. Flash's stack is 55BB, mine deep. Raising to 3x BB (300 total) balances steal and value, considering opponent's history of occasional calls but passivity in spots (e.g., Hand 2, 6). Folding or calling is too weak from button here.

In the small blind with 4c2s offsuit facing a standard Button open to 300, this is an easy fold. Pot is 450 and it costs me 250 to call (≈35.7% equity needed), but 4-2o has very little showdown equity and will be dominated by Elon's opening range. I'm out of position and the Big Blind still acts behind, increasing multi-way risk. 3-betting as a bluff with such an uncoordinated hand is a poor use of deep-stack chips—little fold equity and terrible equity if called. Fold and preserve my stack for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with 8s 2d, one of the weakest hands in poker. Elon has raised from the button. Even though the price to call is relatively small (200 more into a 450 pot), 82o performs very poorly out of position and has almost no post-flop playability. It is a standard fold.

I’m on the button with 4♦9♥. This is a very weak, offsuit hand with poor postflop playability and it’s easy to be dominated by the blinds’ defending ranges. A min-raise to attempt a steal (200) will be called too often by these players and leave me in difficult spots; calling 100 invites multiway action where 94o has little equity. With deep stacks and better spots coming on the button, folding and preserving my stack is the highest EV play here.

I am in the Small Blind with 7h2d, the worst starting hand in Texas Hold'em. Minni, on the button, has already folded. Although I only need to call 50 more to see a flop, 72o has extremely poor playability and equity, even in a blind-vs-blind situation against Elon. While I could attempt a raise to steal the big blind, it is generally better to avoid playing this hand altogether. Folding is the most disciplined play to preserve my stack.

I am holding 8h 4d on the button. This is a very weak starting hand that does not perform well post-flop, especially against the deep stacks of Elon and Minni. Given the current table dynamics and my smaller stack relative to the blinds, I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

8s3h is a complete trash hand: offsuit, low cards with a large gap, no straight or flush potential worth playing. In the small blind facing the big blind after button folds, completing for 50 into a 150 pot gives minimal odds and commits me out of position with a hand that plays terribly post-flop. No reason to raise or call; folding preserves stack for better spots against Minni, who defends wide but this hand has zero equity or playability.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed game, facing a 100 big blind to call into a 150 pot with 4s2h, one of the worst possible hands. No connectivity, low cards, offsuit. Even with position and deep stacks, this hand has zero playability postflop, dominated equity against any calling range, and high risk of losing a big pot if I hit something unlikely. Opponents have shown aggressive tendencies in prior hands, but folding trash here preserves stack for better spots. Standard fold to maximize EV over 150 hands.

Heads-up preflop (SB vs BB) — Elon folded so it’s just me and Flash. 4♣7♣ is a speculative but playable heads-up hand: suited, connected, and has good postflop playability. With deep stacks and only one opponent, I want to take the initiative and apply pressure rather than letting the BB see cheap flops for free. A minimum raise to 200 accomplishes that: it gives fold equity, keeps the pot manageable if called, and preserves a balanced SB opening range. Plan: fold to a large 3-bet (this hand is too weak to defend vs substantial 3-bets), but if called I’ll c-bet favorable flops, control pot size on coordinated boards, and realise equity on safe runouts.

I am in the Big Blind with Q9o. Minni raised from the Small Blind to 200, making it 100 more for me to call into a pot of 300. Q9o is a very standard defending hand in a blind-versus-blind situation, as I am getting 3:1 odds and will have position for the rest of the hand. While I could consider a 3-bet as a bluff, calling is lower variance and allows me to play post-flop with a hand that has decent high-card value and connectivity.

I opened preflop to take the initiative; this Q♠6♣8♠ flop is favorable to a continuation-betting range. My 4♣7♣ is weak (no pair) but has a gutshot to a 5 and backdoor club potential, plus it blocks some low combos. Against a BB caller who flats a wide range, a half-pot c-bet will fold out a lot of pure overcards and unconnected hands, get value from worse draws/pairs, and keep the pot manageable if called. I will bet to apply pressure and gather info; if Flash raises large (significant overbet or shove) I will fold this holding.

I have top pair with a nine kicker on a board of Qs-6c-8s. Minni has led for a half-pot bet of 200. In a blind-versus-blind battle, ranges are wide, and my top pair is currently very strong. However, my kicker is relatively weak, and the board has several draws (straight and flush). Raising now might force out her bluffs while only getting action from hands that beat me (like AQ, KQ, or sets). By calling, I maintain pot control, keep her range wide, and use my position to see how the turn develops. I am getting excellent odds (3:1) to continue.

Turn 5♠ gives me a completed 4-5-6-7-8 straight with 4♣7♣, but it also brings a third spade on board (Q♠ 6♣ 8♠ 5♠). That makes my hand strong but very vulnerable — any two-spade holding in Flash's wide flatting range now has a flush that beats me. Flash called my flop c-bet, so a meaningful portion of his range contains spades. I want to control the pot size and avoid bloating it vs a range that can have flushes. Checking here keeps weaker pairs and bluffs in the pot (I get value from many of them) and lets me fold comfortably to a large raise/overbet that represents a flush. Plan: check now; call a normal value bet, but fold to a large shove/overbet that polarizes to flushes.

I have top pair with a nine kicker on a board where the flush just completed (5 of spades). Minni checked after leading the flop. While I likely have the best hand, the board is very wet with both straight and flush possibilities. Betting here might value-cut myself against her trapping range or force out hands I already beat while only getting called by better. Checking back allows me to control the pot size, avoid a check-raise, and reach showdown or reassess on the river.

Check. I made the 4-5-6-7-8 straight on the turn, but the river completed a fourth spade (Qs 6c 8s 5s 2s). Any spade in Flash’s hand now makes a flush and crushes my straight. Flash called my flop c-bet and then checked the turn and river — that line can be either a weak holding or a slow‑played flush. Betting here would bloat the pot into a range that contains many hands that beat me; a small bet may be called by worse and by flushes, and a larger bet can get me raised off my hand. With pot control and stack preservation my best play is to check to showdown and avoid turning a marginally strong but highly vulnerable hand into a big loss if Flash has a spade.

The board has four spades (Qs, 8s, 5s, 2s) and I hold top pair with no spade. While Minni has checked twice, indicating she likely doesn't have a strong spade or is playing cautiously, my hand has high showdown value but very little value-betting potential. Any single spade in her hand beats me, and it is unlikely she will call a bet with a worse hand that doesn't contain a spade. Checking back is the most prudent play to realize my equity and avoid a check-raise.

On the button in a three-handed pot with Q♠9♠ — a suited, positionally strong hand. With pot only 150 and deep stacks, I should take the initiative rather than limp/call. I’ll open to 3x the big blind (300 total) to apply pressure, isolate the blinds, and give myself the positional advantage postflop. Plan: c-bet favorable flops, control pot size on highly coordinated boards, call small 3-bets occasionally but fold to large 3-bets from Elon or shove only with a much stronger holding. A min-raise or limp gives too much odds and less fold equity, so 300 balances aggression and postflop playability.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Trey offsuit, which is a very weak hand. Minni opened from the Button to 3BB (300). Playing this hand out of position against a big stack is not profitable. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop in BB facing a button raise to 300 after SB folds. Pot 450, calling 200 more for great odds (2.25:1). 67o is a speculative offsuit connector with straight potential and some showdown value. Minni's button range is wide given her aggressive history of frequent preflop raises. Defending the BB wide OOP vs steals is standard, especially deepstacked (165BB). Folding is too tight here; raising risks bloating pot OOP with a marginal hand. Plan to play postflop cautiously, looking for improvement or fold to c-bets.

Flop board 2s 6h 2c gives me two pair (6s and 2s) with a 7 kicker, a very strong hand on this paired, dry board. The paired deuces reduce straight draw possibilities (only 345, very narrow) and make overpairs vulnerable. Minni's button opening range is wide: Ax suited/off, broadway, mid pairs (88-JJ), suited connectors, Ax offsuit. She often raises light from button (seen in hands like #5, #16, #20, #22, #29). I have position-disadvantaged OOP but initiative. Betting builds the pot for value against her Ax (A-high showdown), worse pairs (77-99, some 66/22 unlikely), and floats. Protects against any live draws or pure bluffs. Check might invite free cards or tough river spots OOP. Half-pot bet ~325 sizes well deepstacked (~160BB), gets called by worse, folds junk. Larger like 450 pressures more but risks check-raise from 2x. 400 balances value/protection, ~60% pot. Plan: If raised, evaluate villain strength/history (aggressive but folds to 3b pre sometimes); if called, proceed cautiously turn.

I opened to 300 from the button and Elon just called. Flop 2♠ 6♥ 2♣ and he bets 400 into a 650 pot. I hold Q♠9♠ — two overcards plus backdoor spade equity. I have ~6 clean outs to pair (3 Qs + 3 9s) and a small runner-runner flush possibility — roughly ~24–28% to improve by river. Facing 400 into 650 requires ~38% equity to call purely on pot odds, so a pure showdown call is marginal. However Elon’s preflop calling range is fairly wide and his flop lead will include a lot of missed broadways and medium hands I can exploit by playing in position. The bet is small relative to our deep stacks, so the least-bad line is to call, use position to float and see the turn, and fold to significant turn aggression if I don’t improve. I will not raise — this hand lacks the equity and blockers to force folds reliably and raising risks committing a lot with a weak holding. Call 400 and reassess on the turn (play aggressively if I pair or pick up spade equity, otherwise fold to heavy action).

On the turn Kh, the board is 2s 6h 2c Kh. My 6d7s gives me two pair (sixes and deuces) with a 7 kicker, still a very strong hand on this paired, low board. The king is a reasonable card that hits villain's wide button calling range (KQ, KJ, KTs suited, AK, etc.), but also blank for many Ax high, mid-pairs (77-TT, 88-JJ unlikely to call flop), and live draws (hearts, 45 for gutshot). Minni's flop call of 400 into ~650 pot OOP suggests she has some equity or showdown value: likely Ax (A-high), pocket pairs below 66, suited connectors/draws (34,45,57 etc.), or weaker two-pair like 62/26. Monsters like 22/66/set are possible but in her light opening range they're crushed by better. She stations postflop often (e.g., Hand #2 called huge with ? on flush board, Hand #17 multiway monster pots). Deep stacks (~160BB), so value betting thin is profitable. Checking risks a free river card to her draws (9 outs hearts ~20% equity) or check-raise bluff/value. Betting protects, builds pot against her calling range (Ax, Kx pairs, draws). Sizing: pot 1450, bet ~2/3 pot (1000) gets called by worse (A-high showdown, K-pair, flush draws) while folding pure air. If raised, her history shows aggression but folds pre to 3b sometimes (Hand #1); can reevaluate villain strength. Plan: bet for value/protection, proceed to river strong.

I opened pre and floated the flop with Q♠9♠ planning to fold to heavy turn action if I didn’t improve. The K♥ on the turn doesn’t help me and it also kills any realistic spade flush runner — I now only have three Queens + three Nines = ~6 clean outs (~13% to hit the river). The pot is 2,450 and I must call 1,000, which requires roughly 29% equity to break even (1000/(2450+1000) ≈ 29%). My raw equity (~13%) is far below that, and even if I hit a Q/9 I can still be behind Kx, trips, or two-pair. Raising as a bluff is unattractive: Elon’s line looks like value more often than not, I have poor blockers, and a raise would commit a lot with little fold equity and weak backup equity. Follow my plan to fold to big turn aggression and preserve stack for better spots.

I am on the button with KTo, which is a strong starting hand in a 3-handed game. I have about 50 big blinds. I will open with a standard 3x raise to 300 to take control of the pot and narrow the field or win the blinds immediately.

AK suited is one of the best hands in poker, especially suited for flush potential. Facing a button raise from Flash (4.75BB stack), who raises frequently from late position based on history, a 3-bet is standard to isolate, build the pot for value, and take initiative OOP. Raising to 900 (3x the raise size) is a good size: denies equity to worse hands, pressures Flash's shorter stack, and BB may fold speculative hands. Calling keeps pot multiway OOP, which is worse for AK; folding is too tight preflop.

I’m in the BB with K♢Q♢ facing a Button open to 300 and an SB 3-bet to 900. This hand is too strong to fold but 4-betting here is unattractive: a small 4-bet (min to 1500) won’t reliably isolate and a large 4-bet bloats the pot and can get me committed out of position or facing a 5-bet. Calling 800 (900 total) gives me ~38% break-even equity (800 into ~2100) — KQs has enough equity vs a typical 3‑bet range and plays well postflop. I’ll call to 900, see the flop, and then proceed: if it’s heads‑up I can play aggressively for value; if it stays multiway I’ll pot‑control unless I connect strongly.

I started the action by raising to 300 from the button with KTo. Elon responded with a 3-bet to 900 from the small blind, and Minni cold-called from the big blind. The pot is now 2100 and it costs me 600 more to call. Given the pot odds (600 to win 2100, or 3.5:1), I am getting a very good price to see a flop in position. While KTo is often dominated in a 3-bet pot, the fact that Minni cold-called suggests she might have a medium pair or suited connectors, which my hand performs okay against. I have the button advantage for the rest of the hand. I will call to see the flop.

Preflop 3-bet with AK suited clubs was correct for value and initiative. Flop 2s Qs 6d is fairly dry for my range but drawy overall with spade flush draw and possible straight draws (e.g., JT). I have ace-high with king blocker, best likely high card, and strong blockers to QK, AQ. As preflop aggressor OOP first to act in 2700 pot multiway vs Button caller (short stack 4150) and BB caller (deep). Standard continuation bet for thin value/protection vs worse Ax, Qx, draws that call pre. Check risks giving free cards to draws or allowing free showdown. Bet 2/3 pot (1800) sizes well: gets calls from worse, pressures shorter Flash stack, builds pot if ahead. Avoids overbetting into possible check-raises.

I have top pair (Q) with a very strong K kicker on a fairly dry 2♠ Q♠ 6♦ flop. Elon’s line (PF 3‑bet then a relatively small ~40% pot c‑bet of 1,800 into 4,500) is consistent with a fairly wide range that contains many hands I beat (Ax without a Q, Kx, flush/straight draws, pure bluffs) as well as some Qx/sets. Calling 1,800 gives me ~28.6% pot‑odds and keeps worse hands and draws in the pot while controlling the size against two opponents — raising to 3,600+ bloats the pot, risks an isolating all‑in dynamic (Flash has ~4,150) and can get me 4‑bet/raised off a marginally bloated pot. I’ll call to see a turn, extract value from worse hands, and re‑evaluate versus any turn aggression or a completed spade draw.

I have King-Ten offsuit on a Queen-high board. Elon, who 3-bet pre-flop, has followed up with a large continuation bet of 1800 into a 2700 pot (roughly 2/3 pot), and Minni has already called that bet. This sequence of action is very strong. Elon is representing a hand like AQ, KQ, or an overpair, and Minni calling in the middle likely has a Queen or a strong draw. With only King-high and no real draw other than a back-door straight, I am significantly behind both opponents' ranges. Continuing would be mathematically incorrect and very risky given my remaining stack. I will fold.

Turn As pairs my Ace, giving me top pair top kicker (AcKc on 2s Qs 6d As board), which is strong but vulnerable to completed spade flushes, QQ, sets (22/66/QQ), or two-pair like AQ/KQ that called flop. Minni called my flop cbet OOP after calling my preflop 3-bet, indicating a strong range: likely Qx (QJ+), suited Ax/spades, or monsters. The As completes the spade flush draw and adds straight draw outs (e.g., JT), but also improves my hand significantly from Ace-high. As preflop and flop aggressor OOP heads-up vs deep-stacked Minni in a 6300 pot, continuation betting ~2/3 pot (4200) extracts value from Qx, worse Ax, missed draws, and protects against runner-runner threats while building the pot OOP if ahead. Checking risks giving a free river to draws or inducing bluffs/checks but invites check-raises from strong hands; betting maintains initiative and polarizes vs her calling range. Size 4200 pressures without overcommitting (~28% of my stack), sets up river decisions well.

I have K♦Q♦ on 2♠ Q♠ 6♦ A♠. Pot is 10,500 and Elon has just bet/raised to 4,200; I must call 4,200 (need ~28.6% equity). He 3‑bet preflop and led the flop, so his range is weighted to strong A‑x/AK/AA, two‑pair (AQ), sets (QQ/66/22) and now the turn A (plus the board is three spades) also gives him straightforward spade‑flush hands. Against that value‑heavy range my Q‑top with K‑kicker has poor equity and I need him to be bluffing a large fraction of the time to justify a call. Raising myself to 8,400 would bloat the pot and commit a big chunk of my stack into a spot where I’m often behind and can be 5‑bet/called by better hands — that’s a high‑variance, low‑EV line. Given the sizing, the board card and his line, this is a clear fold — preserve the stack and wait for a better spot.

Preflop on the button with AK suited, a premium hand. Pot is 150 with to-call 100, likely SB limped after posting 50 and BB posted 100. Standard play is to raise for value, position, and to isolate potentially weak blinds. Consistent with my previous opening raise sizes of 300. Flash BB has a shorter stack of 4050, but still deep enough. Minni deep. Raising to 300 (3x BB) builds pot with strong equity.

Ad7d (A7s) in the small blind vs a button open to 300. Pot is 450 and I need to put 250 more (300 total) to call. A7s is too good to fold — it has nut-A potential and a suit for flush/straight equity — but I am out of position to both the button and the big blind. The min-re-raise to 500 is small and unlikely to fold out the big blind (Flash has ~4050) and would often create a multiway pot where I’m OOP with a marginal hand. Elon opens frequently and likely has a relatively wide range, so calling preserves my equity and keeps the pot under control. Plan: call to see the flop (300 total). If I hit an Ace or diamond draws I’ll continue aggressively; if I completely miss I’ll control the pot and fold to heavy aggression.

I am in the Big Blind with 8h Th and have been offered excellent pot odds to see a flop. Elon raised from the Button and Minni called from the Small Blind. Calling 200 more into a pot of 700 requires only about 22% equity. T8h is a strong enough speculative hand to defend the Big Blind in a multi-way pot, especially since I'll be closing the action.

Check. On 9♠‑9♦‑Q♠ in a three‑way pot I have only Ace‑high with A♦7♦ and a very remote backdoor diamond flush (need both turn+river). I’m out of position to the opener (Elon) and a calling big blind (Flash), so a c‑bet here is unlikely to fold enough better hands (Qx, 9x, pocket pairs) to be profitable and will just get me raised or priced out. Priority is pot control and preserving stack equity. Plan: check and evaluate turns — fold to significant aggression (raises or large C‑bets), call small C‑bets to see a safe turn if villain is floating, and only become aggressive if I pair the Ace, complete a diamond draw, or get a good read to bluff later.

The flop is 9s 9d Qs. I am in the Big Blind with 10h 8h. The pot is 900 and Minni (Small Blind) has checked. Elon (Button) was the pre-flop raiser. My hand has no direct connection to this board except for a backdoor straight draw and potentially some overcards to the nines, though the Queen is out. Given the multi-way nature and the fact that I'm closing the action on this street if I check, checking is the standard play to see what the original raiser does. I have no reason to bet into two players on this board with air.

Flop of 9s9dQs completely misses my AKcc, leaving me with ace-high and no draws. However, I have position, the betting lead, and excellent blockers to AA, KK, AQ, and KQ—the primary value hands that crush the calling ranges of SB and BB after calling my button raise preflop. The paired board reduces the likelihood of monsters like QQ or sets in their ranges (they'd often 3-bet pre), and both checks suggest weakness or traps, but traps are less common. This is a standard spot to barrel for thin value/protection/ fold equity against speculative calling ranges (Qx, JTs, lower pairs, draws). Betting 2/3 pot (600) builds the pot when ahead, applies pressure on Flash's shorter stack (SPR ~4:1 post-bet), and denies equity to draws like KsJs or 8T flush/straight draws. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards or draws that could turn ahead. Consistent with aggressive button play from history.

I have Ad7d on 9s-9d-Qs. I checked the flop to control the pot — I only have Ace-high and an extremely remote runner-runner diamond flush. Elon (the button opener) bets 600 into a 1500 pot (~40% pot). That size is a small C-bet in a multiway pot and indicates a fairly wide range (thin value + bluffs). Calling here preserves stack equity and buys me a free/look at the turn where I can fold to real aggression or continue if I improve. Raising OOP with just Ace-high is too risky and would commit chips unnecessarily, and folding now surrenders too much equity vs his wide c-bet range. Plan: call 600 to see the turn; fold to a large raise from either opponent unless I pair the Ace or improve to a real draw or better.

On a board of 9s 9d Qs, Elon (the pre-flop raiser) has bet 600 into a 900 pot. Minni, who is in the Small Blind, has already called. My hand, 10h 8h, is a complete miss. While the pot odds are roughly 4.5 to 1 (600 to win a total pot of 2700), my hand has almost no equity against two ranges that are willing to continue on this board. Elon is betting into two people, and Minni has signaled strength or a strong draw by calling. I have no pair, no real draw besides a gutshot if a Jack comes (and even then, I lose to any Q, 9, or higher straight), and I am out of position to both players. Folding is the only sensible move here to preserve my remaining 38.5 big blinds.

Turn 2♦ gives me Ad7d a four-card diamond draw plus Ace-high. I have 9 diamond outs to a flush and three non‑diamond Aces (roughly 12 unique outs → ~26% to improve on the river). Pot is 2,100 and Elon has shown a wide continuing range with a small flop c-bet, so a medium-sized semi-bluff here both has fold equity against many Qx/overcards and lets me build the pot when I complete my draw. I will bet a little over half the pot to apply pressure but keep the commitment controlled. If Elon raises big I will fold (or re-evaluate only if the raise is small enough to give correct pot odds for the draw); if he calls I can realize my equity on the river and proceed accordingly.

Facing a turn donk bet of 1200 into 3300 after Minni check-called flop on paired 9s9dQs board. My AKc is still ace-high no pair no draw, pure bluff catcher at best. Turn 2d is a blank that doesn't help me and doesn't complete major draws (spade flush draw still live but her check-call flop suggests not a strong draw). Minni's line—calling button raise pre, check-call flop c-bet, now leading turn—represents significant strength: likely Qx (QJ/QT suited spades), 9x/set, or Ax that didn't want to check-raise flop. Similar board in Hand 15 (9h9dQc) where she check-called small flop bet but folded to turn bet, but here she leads after larger flop bet, indicating more commitment/strength. Raising as a bluff has fold equity given her history folding to aggression (e.g., Hands 3,15,20), but her donk turn upgrades her range to value-heavy; getting called or re-raised loses my stack. Calling builds pot OOP with minimal equity (overcards unlikely to be clean outs vs her range) and low SPR (~3:1 post-call), facing river jam likely. Folding preserves stack for better spots; villain can have 9x/Qx/AQ often, and I have <25% equity vs that. Consistent with disciplined play avoiding marginal hero calls vs aggressive villains.

Button open with 8♠9♣ in a 3-handed spot is a clear raising candidate — the hand has good connectivity and plays very well in position. I want to apply pressure and build the pot when I have positional advantage, so I’ll open to a standard 3x BB (300). That sizing preserves postflop maneuverability, gives fold equity versus marginal blind ranges, and is consistent with table norms. If I get a single caller I’ll c-bet frequently on dry flops and use position to take the pot; if it becomes multiway I’ll play more cautiously. If Elon (deep, aggressive) 3-bets big I’ll usually fold this hand (dominated/poor implied odds); I may consider calling a smallish 3-bet depending on sizing and Flash’s reaction.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten-Deuce offsuit (T2o). Minni, the Button, has raised to 300. Given the extreme weakness of my hand and the fact that I'm out of position against a strong stack, there is no reason to continue. I'll fold and preserve my 3800 chips for a better starting hand.

Ts2c is a very weak offsuit hand with minimal playability, no straight or flush potential, and dominated against a typical button raising range from Minni, who has been aggressive preflop. In the big blind, calling 200 into a 450 pot out of position offers poor implied odds and equity (likely under 30% vs. a wide range). Folding preserves stack for better spots.

In the Button position with Jack-Seven offsuit, this is a marginal hand. The action is currently folded to me with the blinds yet to act. While J7o is normally a fold from early or middle positions, in a three-handed game from the button, it can occasionally be opened or folded depending on the table dynamics. Looking at the history, Elon (SB) has been aggressive in 3-betting and Minni (BB) has been fairly active in defending or leading. Given my relatively short stack of 3800 compared to the blinds (19975 and 16075), I need to be selective about the pots I enter to avoid being bled dry or forced into difficult spots post-flop with weak holdings. Folding is the safest and most standard play here to preserve chips for a better spot.

Preflop from the small blind facing a fold from Flash and only the big blind behind. My hand is 3c6c, a weak suited connector with poor playability and low equity heads-up or multiway. Completing the blind offers minimal fold equity against a defending big blind, and the pot odds (calling 50 into 200) do not justify playing a speculative hand this weak from out of position postflop. Opponents have shown aggressive tendencies in prior hands, but folding trash preserves my deep stack (19,975) for better spots in this multi-table cash game simulation aiming to maximize stack over 150 hands.

Preflop on the button in a 3-handed game with 64o offsuit, a weak speculative hand with poor playability. Position is good, but the hand lacks high card strength, suitedness, or strong connectivity. Facing blinds with deep stacks except Flash who is shorter at 3700, but calling 100 OOP into a pot of 150 risks playing postflop with a weak range. Standard play is to fold trash hands preflop to maintain discipline and wait for better spots to maximize EV over 150 hands.

Heads-up preflop versus the big blind I want to take the initiative with a playable, suited broadway-ish hand. Q♥9♥ is worth opening here: it has good playability postflop (quarter/flush + straight potential) and will realize equity well against a wide defending range. Pot math: pot is 150; raising to 300 (3x the BB) means I add 250 now, making the pot 400 and giving the BB a 200 call for ~33% odds — enough fold equity to steal many marginal hands while charging draws. Flash has shown a mix of calls and folds; a 3x open pressures his weaker holdings but keeps my range wide and manageable. Plan: if called, play aggressively on favorable flops and use my positional/in-form advantage to apply pressure; if he 3-bets large, fold Q9s to avoid marginal dominated situations against a tighter 3-bet range.

I am in the Big Blind with K d 3 h. Minni has raised from the Small Blind to 300. This is a blind-versus-blind situation where Minni's range is likely quite wide. However, K3o is a relatively weak hand even in this context. I have a stack of 3700 (37 Big Blinds), while Minni has a much larger stack. Calling 200 more into a 400 pot gives me 3:1 odds, which is tempting, but K3o plays poorly post-flop and often gets dominated. Given my short stack relative to the table, I should avoid marginal spots out of position. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

7♦3♣ is a very weak, easily-dominated hand. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop — you need roughly 100/(150+100) ≈ 40% equity to make a pure call break-even. Against two defending ranges from the small blind and a large-stacked, often-defending big blind (Elon) I won’t have anywhere near that equity. Raising to 200 to try to steal risks getting called by better hands and playing a difficult multiway pot out of position post‑flop. With a 16k+ stack and many hands left in the session, preserving chips for real position/steal opportunities or playable holdings is the correct long‑term choice. Fold and wait for a stronger spot on the button.

With Minni folding on the button, it's a heads-up situation between the blinds. Queen-Jack offsuit is a very strong hand in a blind vs. blind encounter. I will raise to 300 to exert pressure on Elon, gain the initiative, and potentially win the pot immediately without seeing a flop.

9d3s is a complete trash hand: offsuit, disconnected, low cards, no speculative value. Facing a standard raise from SB (Flash with 3400 stack ~34BB), pot odds are 200 to call into 400 (2:1), but OOP post-flop with this hand is disastrous against any continuation. No fold equity or implied odds justify calling. Folding preserves stack for better spots.

I have pocket tens (Tc Td) on the button. This is a very strong hand, especially in a 3-handed game. My stack is quite short (3800 chips, 38 big blinds), so I want to play aggressively to potentially double up or take down the blinds. A standard open raise on the button is 3x the big blind.

Preflop in SB with A2o facing a button raise from Flash (35BB stack). A2 offsuit is a weak hand OOP, with poor postflop playability against a wide button range. Pot odds (250 to call into 450) are decent but not enough to justify calling given position and hand strength. Folding preserves stack for better spots in a long 150-hand game.

I have J♦4♠ in the big blind facing a button open to 300 (I must call 200). This is a marginal, easily dominated hand with poor playability OOP — no suit, weak kicker, few good flops. Pot odds to call (200 into 450) require ~31% equity, but against a BTN opening range J4o will often be dominated or have insufficient equity to comfortably play multi-street out of position with ~35bb effective stacks. A light 3-bet to 500 is technically possible as a bluff, but I don’t have the hand or a strong read to make that profitable here and it bloats the pot OOP. I’ll preserve my stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

Folded to me on the button in a 3-handed game after SB Minni folded. Facing only BB Flash with 38.5BB stack. 8c6s is a strong suited connector playable from the button with position. Standard play is to raise for value, steal blinds, or play post-flop profitably. Raising to 3x the BB (300 total) is a standard sizing to build the pot or take it down preflop. Good playability and equity against BB defending range.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. J♣9♠ in the small blind is a marginal, often-dominated hand out of position. Calling 250 to see a flop would leave me OOP vs the button (and possibly the BB) in a multiway pot with poor playability; the immediate pot odds (450:250 ≈ 1.8:1) don’t justify calling into a likely better opening range. A minimum 3-bet to 500 is just a thin bluff here and will be called often enough by both opponents, creating difficult postflop decisions. I’ll fold and preserve my deep stack for a better, more profitable spot.

I am in the Big Blind with Qs2s, which is a suited gapper but relatively weak. Elon on the Button raised to 300. I need to call 200 more into a pot of 450. The pot odds are 2.25:1. While Q2s isn't a premium hand, suited Queens have some playability in a heads-up situation against a button opening range, especially since I'm closing the action. However, my stack is relatively short compared to the others (3850), and I should be cautious about playing weak hands out of position. Looking at previous hands, Elon is quite active. Given the price, I will call to see a flop, hoping to hit a flush draw or a strong pair, but I'll be prepared to fold if the board doesn't improve my hand significantly.

I am in the Big Blind with Qs2s and the flop is 7h 4d Js. I have nothing but a backdoor flush draw and an overcard Queen. The pre-flop raiser, Elon, is in position and has been active. Since I am first to act on this flop, checking is the standard play with this range. I have no reason to lead here given the board texture doesn't favor my defending range significantly and my hand has very little equity. I will check and see what Elon does.

On the flop of 7h4dJs, a dry rainbow board, Flash checks after calling preflop from the BB. As the button raiser with position, this is a standard spot to c-bet for value, protection, and fold equity against a wide defending range. My 8c6s has a gutshot straight draw to the 5 (for 4-5-6-7-8), backdoor spade draw (6s with Js on board), and 8-overcard potential. Flash's history shows a mix of calling and folding to postflop aggression, but checked flops are exploitable. Pot 650, betting ~half pot (300) builds it appropriately without overcommitting, maintaining balance and pressure with deep stacks (effective ~36BB for Flash). Checking back risks giving free cards and losing initiative.

I am in the Big Blind with Queen-high (Qs 2s) on a 7h 4d Js flop. After checking to the pre-flop aggressor, Elon, he has placed a continuation bet of 300 into a 650 pot.
